questions.question: In the Body Paragraph 2 - Freedom's Daughters box, write a topic sentence that identifies the main idea the author develops about Barbara Johns' actions.

responses.content: Claudette Colvin’s courageous act of rebellion on the bus was a foundational moment that ignited the Montgomery bus boycott. Her rejection to give up her seat to a white passenger, despite being only a 15 year old, demonstrated overall bravery in the face of unjust segregation laws. This act of defiance, though not the one that ultimately became the spark for the boycott served as a powerful example for resistance. Claudette’s arrest, occurring nine months before Rosa Parks more widely publicized act, highlighted the deep-seated madness within the black community. While civil rights leaders initially hesitated to use Claudette’s case due to concerns about her public image, her willingness to stand up against injustice resonated with many. This early instance of resistance, even if not immediately amplified, laid groundwork by showcasing the possibility of challenging segregation openly this sustained and impactful Montgomery buss boycott.
